paper_title: A shallow compaction model for Holocene Mississippi Delta sediments

methods.description: Decompaction of the actual core allows computation of the average accretion rate over time spans during the Holocene. This is achieved by combining the relationship between void index and depth with an age-depth model.

steps.description: Collect oedometric test data from the Myrtle Grove core I to establish the void index vs. depth relationship.
steps.searchable_summary: collect oedometric test data for void index vs. depth relationship
inputs: Myrtle Grove core I oedometric test data

steps.description: Collect 14C datings of organic remnants at different elevations within the sedimentary column to establish an age-depth model.
Compute the decompacted thickness of the soil column between specified depths using the void index and age-depth model.
steps.searchable_summary: compute decompacted thickness using void index and age-depth model

steps.description: Calculate the average accretion rate within a specified time interval using deposition times and decompacted thickness.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate average accretion rate using deposition times and decompacted thickness

methods.description: Simulates accretion and consolidation of the Myrtle Grove core I using the NATSUB-2D numerical model, which solves a coupled system of equations for 2-D groundwater flow and 1-D consolidation.

steps.description: Set up the NATSUB-2D model to simulate accretion and consolidation processes over a compaction-free Pleistocene basement.
steps.searchable_summary: set up NATSUB-2D model for accretion and consolidation simulation

steps.description: Compute pore overpressure evolution using the groundwater flow model during deposition of unit 1.
steps.searchable_summary: compute pore overpressure using groundwater flow model

steps.description: Simulate consolidation of unit 1 due to over-pressure dissipation and deposition of unit 2.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate consolidation of unit 1 and deposition of unit 2

steps.description: Include contributions of deep subsidence factors such as glacial isostatic adjustment and regional sediment loading.
steps.searchable_summary: include deep subsidence factors in model

steps.description: Solve the flow equations using a 1-D framework for the Holocene sequence of the Myrtle Grove core I.
Implement numerical solution using Finite Element discretization, back Euler method for time integration, and fixed-point iteration for material non-linearity.
steps.searchable_summary: implement numerical solution with FE discretization and back Euler method

steps.description: Use a Lagrangian approach with a dynamic mesh to account for large deformations without geometric non-linearity.
steps.searchable_summary: use Lagrangian approach with dynamic mesh for large deformations

steps.description: Adopt a constant time step of 10 years and add a new finite element when soil accretion reaches 10 cm.
steps.searchable_summary: adopt constant time step and add finite element for soil accretion
